question: What happens when you roll a 7?
expected_answer: No one receives resources. Every player with more than 7 resource cards must discard half (rounded down). Then the player who rolled must move the robber to any other terrain hex and steal one resource card at random from a player with a settlement or city adjacent to that hex.
model_answer: When you roll a "7," none of the players receive resources. Each player with more than 7 resource cards must discard half of them. Then, you must move the robber to another terrain hex, blocking its resource production. After moving the robber, you steal 1 resource card at random from a player with a settlement or city adjacent to the new hex. Your turn continues with the trade phase. This is according to [Almanac: ROLLING A "7" AND ACTIVATING THE ROBBER].

question: I just built a settlement on a harbor this turn. Can I immediately use that harbor's favorable trade rate?
expected_answer: In standard play (separate trade and build phases), no — you build during the build phase after trading is done, so you cannot trade again. However, using the Combined Trade/Build Phase variant (recommended for experienced players), you can trade and build in any order during your turn, so yes — the rules explicitly state you can use a harbor on the same turn you build a settlement there.
model_answer: Yes, you can immediately use that harbor's favorable trade rate. According to [Almanac: HARBORS], you must build a settlement on a coastal intersection which borders the harbor to control it, and once you have done so, you can trade more favorably during your trade phase.

question: My road is 6 segments long and holds the Longest Road card. An opponent builds a settlement in the middle of it, breaking it into two branches of 4 and 2. Do I lose the Longest Road card?
expected_answer: Your road is broken. You now count only your longest continuous branch, which is 4 segments. Since 4 is less than 5, you no longer have a qualifying road at all. If another player already has a longer continuous road, they take the card. If two or more players now tie for the longest road, the card is set aside until one player has a clearly longer road of at least 5 segments.
model_answer:
According to the rules, if your longest road is broken and you are tied for the longest road, you still keep the "Longest Road" card. However, since your road is now broken into two branches of 4 and 2, you no longer have a continuous road of at least 5 segments. In this case, if two or more players tie for the new longest road, you must set the "Longest Road" card aside. Therefore, you do lose the Longest Road card. 

This is supported by the following excerpt: "if you no longer have the longest road, but two or more players tie for the new longest road, set the 'Longest Road' card aside." [Almanac: LONGEST ROAD]
